task_completion.description: Verify the Liverpool Cancer iChart website is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the Liverpool Cancer iChart homepage or search interface.

task_completion.description: Verify 'Enzalutamide' is entered or selected as the cancer drug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Enzalutamide' is visible in the cancer drug input field or selection list.

task_completion.description: Verify 'Warfarin' is entered or selected as the co-medication.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Warfarin' is visible in the co-medication input field or selection list.

task_completion.description: Verify the interaction search result is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A results page or table showing the interaction between Enzalutamide and Warfarin, which should be indicated as a RED (Major) interaction.

task_completion.description: Verify navigation to the Interaction Details page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the specific, expanded interaction details page for Enzalutamide and Warfarin, rather than just the summary table.

task_completion.description: Verify the full clinical recommendation is displayed on screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Detailed clinical text explaining the interaction is visible, which should include terms like 'INR', 'monitor', 'CYP2C9', 'inducer', or 'bleed'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a web browser to access the genuine Liverpool Cancer iChart website.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser UI with the correct URL (e.g., cancer-druginteractions.org) is visible, not a local HTML file or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the GUI rather than using terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows running curl, python scripts, or API requests to fetch the interaction data.

integrity.description: Verify the interaction details are from the actual application interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The clinical recommendation is displayed within the site's standard styling and layout, not as raw text pasted into a notepad or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Cancer drug: Enzalutamide. Co-medication: Warfarin. Expected interaction severity: RED (Major Interaction). The clinical recommendation should mention keywords such as 'CYP2C9', 'inducer', 'induction', 'monitor', 'INR', or 'bleed', as Enzalutamide induces CYP2C9, reducing warfarin's anticoagulant effect and requiring intensive INR monitoring.
